name: ⛪ Church Ministry
tagline: AI-powered tools for Sunday School, pastoral care, sermons, Bible study & church administration
description: A complete ministry intelligence pack for churches of all sizes and denominations. Grace — the central coordinator — routes every request to the right specialist worker, each running in its own isolated container. Sermon prep, Sunday School lessons, Bible study, pastoral care, worship planning, church administration, communications, and prayer — all served by a team of specialist agents that understand the unique demands of church life.

agents.name: Grace — Church Ministry Coordinator
agents.description: The entry point for all church ministry work. Grace greets with warmth, discerns the ministry need, and spawns the right specialist worker — or answers directly for simple questions. She holds the human-review gate before any output reaches a congregation.

agents.name: The Preacher — Sermon Preparation
agents.description: Full sermon preparation: passage exegesis, theological research, three-point outlines with illustrations, application points, and closing prayer prompts. Spawned by Grace for any preaching request.

agents.name: The Teacher — Sunday School
agents.description: Complete age-appropriate Sunday School lessons: biblical teaching, crafts, memory verses, discussion questions, and parent take-home notes. Covers nursery through senior high.

agents.name: The Scholar — Bible Study
agents.description: Deep passage study, multi-week series outlines, discussion question banks, cross-references, and historical-cultural context for small group facilitators.

agents.name: The Shepherd — Pastoral Care
agents.description: Compassionate care support: visit preparation, response drafts, grief support, crisis detection, follow-up plans, and professional referral guidance. Uses the most capable model for sensitive human situations.

agents.name: The Worshipper — Worship Planning
agents.description: Liturgically coherent service planning: song sets aligned to the sermon theme, service flow, Scripture readings, liturgical responses, and seasonal planning for Advent, Lent, Easter, and Pentecost.

agents.name: The Herald — Communications
agents.description: Church communications across every channel: social media posts, newsletter articles, welcome emails, event invitations, prayer request circulars, and testimony features — all requiring human review before release.

agents.name: The Intercessor — Prayer & Contemplation
agents.description: Structured prayer support: prayer meeting guides, intercession lists, personal journal frameworks (ACTS model), Lectio Divina guides, fasting plans, and prayer chain templates.

agents.name: The Steward — Church Administration
agents.description: Complete church administration in a single worker: routes internally across event planning, bulletins, volunteers, finance, membership, facility bookings, meeting minutes, and calendar management. Returns a unified, review-ready package.

agents.name: Scripture & Theology Researcher
agents.description: Deep passage research: original language context (Greek/Hebrew word meanings), historical background, theological frameworks across denominations, and commentary synthesis. Spawned in parallel by Grace alongside The Preacher for research-heavy sermons.

agents.name: Illustration & Story Miner
agents.description: Generates contemporary illustrations, analogies, parables, and real-world stories that connect Scripture to modern congregational experience. Fast, focused, output-only worker.

agents.name: Theological Reviewer
agents.description: Reviews sermon drafts for theological accuracy, denominational alignment, pastoral sensitivity, and balance. Returns a structured review with flagged issues and suggested corrections.

agents.name: Lesson Structure Designer
agents.description: Structures Sunday School lessons with precise timing, age-appropriate learning objectives, and biblical scaffolding. Outputs a complete timed lesson plan skeleton for The Teacher to fill.

agents.name: Creative Activity Generator
agents.description: Generates crafts, drama scripts, interactive games, and hands-on activities matched to the lesson theme and age group. Includes materials list and step-by-step facilitation guide.

agents.name: Age-Appropriate Content Adapter
agents.description: Rewrites theological content at the right cognitive and emotional level for each age band: nursery (2–4), kindergarten (5–6), junior (7–9), primary (10–12), youth (13–17).

agents.name: Passage Deep-Dive Analyst
agents.description: Verse-by-verse passage analysis: Greek/Hebrew word meanings, narrative structure, theological themes, intertextual links, and canonical placement. The most rigorous exegetical worker in the pack.

agents.name: Discussion Question Generator
agents.description: Creates observation, interpretation, and application questions at multiple depth levels — from ice-breaker to deep theological reflection — for small group discussion across varying spiritual maturity levels.

agents.name: Historical-Cultural Context Researcher
agents.description: Provides the historical, cultural, geographical, and political context that illuminates why a passage was written and what it meant to its original audience — essential for accurate interpretation.

agents.name: Pastoral Care Response Drafter
agents.description: Drafts compassionate, theologically grounded responses for pastoral conversations, care visit follow-up emails, and bereavement letters. Tone-matched to the situation — grief, illness, conflict, or spiritual dryness.

agents.name: Crisis Detection & Response
agents.description: Activated immediately when any message signals self-harm, abuse, acute mental health crisis, or immediate danger. Provides crisis resources, safe pastoral response language, and clear escalation steps to a human pastor. Uses the most capable model — safety is never compromised.

agents.name: Professional Referral Advisor
agents.description: Identifies when a pastoral situation requires clinical, legal, or social services referral and provides compassionate, practical guidance on making the handoff without abandoning the member.

agents.name: The Organiser — Event Planning
agents.description: Complete event planning packages: project timeline, team structure, budget framework, risk assessment, accessibility checklist, run sheet, and post-event review template. Handles prayer breakfasts through 500-person community outreach events.

agents.name: The Publisher — Bulletin & Newsletter
agents.description: Assembles complete church bulletins, orders of service, special occasion programs (Christmas, Easter, Baptism, Funeral), and monthly newsletters from provided inputs. Produces print-ready and digital-ready formatted output with a QA checklist.

agents.name: The Coordinator — Volunteer Management
agents.description: Builds and manages volunteer rotas, generates role descriptions, tracks DBS/safeguarding compliance flags, creates onboarding workflows, monitors burnout risk indicators, and designs appreciation programmes for ministry teams.

agents.name: The Treasurer's Assistant — Finance
agents.description: Generates budget frameworks, treasurer report templates, giving summary formats, Gift Aid administration guidance (UK), stewardship campaign materials, and grant application frameworks. Never stores actual financial figures — templates only.

agents.name: The Welcomer — Membership & Visitors
agents.description: Manages the full visitor-to-member journey: connection card templates, follow-up sequences, membership pathway documents, transfer letters, inactive member outreach scripts, and small group matching frameworks. No personal member data stored.

agents.name: The Keeper — Facility Booking
agents.description: Manages church space: internal booking forms, external hire agreements, pricing frameworks, room setup guides, key management protocols, facility inspection checklists, and accessibility compliance reviews.

agents.name: The Scribe — Meeting Secretary
agents.description: Produces professional meeting documentation for all governance levels: agenda builders, structured minutes templates, action log management, AGM complete packages (notice, annual report, accounts framework), and governance health checks.

agents.name: The Timekeeper — Calendar Management
agents.description: Plans the full ministry year: annual calendar aligned to liturgical and civil calendars, monthly planning sheets, conflict detection, seasonal countdown timelines (Christmas, Easter, Lent), and multi-format output (digital, print, .ics).

inspiration_prompts.title: Sermon on Lazarus
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Pastor preparing a message for a congregation facing grief and loss
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Help me prepare a full sermon outline on John 11 (the raising of Lazarus) for a congregation that has experienced three bereavements this month. I want to preach on resurrection hope without minimising real grief.

inspiration_prompts.title: Sunday School: David & Goliath
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Teacher preparing a lesson for 7–9 year olds with a craft activity
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Create a complete Sunday School lesson on David and Goliath for children aged 7–9. Include a clear biblical teaching, a sling-and-stone craft activity using paper cups and string, a memory verse (1 Samuel 17:47), and a parent take-home note.

inspiration_prompts.title: 6-Week Psalms of Ascent Study
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Small group leader launching a Lent study series
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Design a 6-week small group Bible study series on the Psalms of Ascent (Psalms 120–134) for Lent. Include a series overview, weekly passage focus, historical-cultural context, 5 discussion questions per week (observation, interpretation, application), and a closing prayer guide.

inspiration_prompts.title: Pastoral Visit: Bereavement
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Pastoral care worker preparing to visit a widow three weeks after loss
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Help me prepare for a pastoral care visit with a 70-year-old widow whose husband of 48 years passed three weeks ago. She has expressed feeling abandoned by God. What should I bring, what do I say, what Scriptures are helpful, and what does the follow-up plan look like over the next 3 months?

inspiration_prompts.title: Pentecost Sunday Worship Set
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Worship leader planning a service aligned to a sermon on Acts 2
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Plan a full worship set for Pentecost Sunday aligned to a sermon on Acts 2 (the coming of the Holy Spirit). Include song suggestions from classic hymns and contemporary worship, service flow timing, a responsive liturgical reading, and a moment of reflection before the sermon.

inspiration_prompts.title: Christmas Dinner for 150
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Church secretary planning a community outreach event 6 weeks out
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Create a complete planning package for a church community Christmas dinner for 150 people, 6 weeks from today. Include: project timeline with weekly milestones, volunteer team structure with role descriptions, budget framework, dietary accommodation checklist, run sheet for the evening, and a debrief template.

inspiration_prompts.title: Sunday Bulletin
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Administrator assembling this week's order of service
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Build this Sunday's bulletin. Sermon: 'Walking on Water — Faith in the Storm' from Matthew 14:22–33, preached by Pastor James. Worship led by Sarah Chen. Announcements: Alpha Course starts Tuesday 7pm, Harvest Thanksgiving Lunch on Sunday 23rd, new Friday evening small group forming. Include a welcome for first-time visitors and a congregational prayer section.

inspiration_prompts.title: Midweek Prayer Meeting
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Prayer meeting leader preparing a 45-minute intercession guide
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Create a structured 45-minute prayer meeting guide for our midweek prayer group of 12 people. Focus: intercession for our city, a church family facing a serious illness, and our upcoming Alpha Course launch. Include Scripture prompts for each station, a Lectio Divina opening, and a closing commissioning prayer.

inspiration_prompts.title: Welcome Team Rota
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Volunteer coordinator building a 4-week Sunday morning rota
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Create a 4-week volunteer rota for our Welcome Team. We have 10 volunteers: Sarah, James, Mary, Tom, Grace, David, Ruth, Peter, Hannah, John. We need 3 people each Sunday with a rotating team lead. Include a WhatsApp message template to send each team their week's assignment.

inspiration_prompts.title: Annual Budget Framework
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Treasurer presenting to the church AGM
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Generate a complete annual church budget framework template for a congregation of around 200 adults meeting in a rented hall. Include all income categories (regular giving, events, hall hire, designated funds) and expenditure categories (ministry, staff, premises, mission, administration). Format it for presentation at our AGM.

inspiration_prompts.title: Visitor Follow-Up Journey
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Membership coordinator building a 3-month follow-up sequence
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Design a complete 3-month visitor follow-up sequence for first-time guests. Include: connection card template, same-week welcome email, 2-week personal follow-up call script, 1-month invitation to newcomers' lunch, and a clear pathway from visitor to church member. All communication templates included.

inspiration_prompts.title: Elders Meeting Agenda
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Church secretary preparing for the monthly leadership meeting
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Create a formal agenda for our monthly elders meeting. Main discussion items: (1) Review of the Alpha Course launch — numbers, team feedback, follow-up plan; (2) Youth worker appointment process — shortlist and interview dates; (3) Q3 financial report presentation; (4) Safeguarding policy annual review. Include time allocations and a minutes template.

inspiration_prompts.title: First-Time Visitor Welcome Email
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Communications team reaching out to last Sunday's new guests
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Write a warm, genuine welcome email for first-time visitors who attended our Sunday service last week. Tone: friendly neighbour, not corporate. Include: gratitude for visiting, brief about who we are, 3 ways to take a next step (small group, newcomers' lunch, Alpha), and a personal sign-off from the pastor. No jargon.

inspiration_prompts.title: Hall Hire Agreement
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Administrator creating a legal template for external room hire
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Generate a professional external hall hire agreement template for our church building. We hire out a 100-person hall, a smaller meeting room, and a commercial kitchen. Include: booking terms, cancellation policy (with 3 tiers), safeguarding requirements for groups with children, insurance requirements, a damage deposit clause, and key collection procedures.

inspiration_prompts.title: Annual Ministry Calendar
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Leadership team planning the coming church year
inspiration_prompts.prompt: Help us plan our annual ministry calendar. Key commitments: Lent sermon series on the Psalms starting Ash Wednesday, Easter services (Good Friday and Easter Sunday), Alpha Course launching in September, Harvest Thanksgiving in October, Christmas services in December. Build a month-by-month calendar with 6-week preparation lead times flagged for each major event.

inspiration_prompts.title: Sermon Research: Feeding of the 5000
inspiration_prompts.scenario: Pastor wanting deep exegetical support before writing an outline
inspiration_prompts.prompt: I am preaching on the Feeding of the 5000 (John 6:1–15) in two weeks. Before I write my outline, I need deep research: the significance of Passover timing (v.4), the Greek word for 'boy' (paidarion) and what it tells us about the miracle's texture, parallels with Moses and Elijah, how John's account differs from the Synoptics, and 3 theologically sound application angles for today.
